codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested env-gated Docker integration tests (start/stop/is_running/adoption) and they do skip gracefully when ROOKERY_INTEGRATION is unset, but the implementation has a blocking reliability flaw when integration mode is enabled.

issues.description: All four integration tests share the same fixed host port (19876) via integration_test_config while each test is a normal #[tokio::test] (lines 2213, 2290, 2366, 2410) that can run concurrently under cargo test. When ROOKERY_INTEGRATION=1, parallel execution can cause Docker port-binding conflicts and nondeterministic failures, violating the expected behavior that enabled integration tests pass.
The integration helper pins docker_image to the floating tag vllm/vllm-openai:latest, which can change behavior over time and reduce reproducibility of integration test outcomes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance documents env-gating for integration tests but does not document handling shared resources (ports/docker project isolation) for parallel test execution, which likely contributed to the fixed-port parallelism bug.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md:67 documents only env gating; backend.rs:2155 fixes port 19876 while integration tests at backend.rs:2213,2290,2366,2410 are parallelizable tokio tests.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking test-design issue: integration tests use a shared fixed port and can race under parallel cargo test runs when ROOKERY_INTEGRATION=1. Non-blocking reproducibility concern: floating Docker image tag.
